- **Focus**: P2P network boundary validation, input validation at trust boundary

          "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the extra_data field in block headers properly validated to not exceed 32 bytes at P2P ingress?",
          "severity": "Medium",
          "mindset": "Boundary Guard",
          "is_boundary_check": true,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Identify all P2P block reception handlers. 2. Trace block header deserialization to locate extra_data field extraction. 3. Verify length validation occurs BEFORE any processing of extra_data content. 4. Submit block with extra_data of exactly 32 bytes - verify acceptance. 5. Submit block with extra_data of 33 bytes - verify rejection. 6. Submit block with extra_data of 0 bytes - verify handling. 7. Submit block with extra_data at maximum uint64 length value - verify safe rejection without buffer overflow. 8. Verify error response does not leak internal state. 9. Check that validation occurs at the earliest possible point in the processing pipeline.",
          "bug_class": "Input Validation",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0053, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Subgraph: SG-004. Invariant property for extra_data length. Oversized extra_data could cause buffer issues or consensus splits."
        },
        {
          "check_id": "CHK-0053-02",
          "property_id": "PROP-0053",
          "title": "Is the 32-byte extra_data length invariant maintained consistently across all block validation paths?",
          "severity": "Medium",
          "mindset": "Formal Verification Engineer",
          "is_boundary_check": false,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Enumerate all code paths that process block headers (P2P, RPC, local block production, import). 2. Verify each path enforces the 32-byte limit. 3. Check for bypass conditions (special block types, genesis block, etc.). 4. Verify the comparison operator is correct (<=32, not <32 or <33). 5. Check for integer overflow in length comparisons. 6. Test blocks created through alternative paths (snap sync, fast sync, etc.).",
          "bug_class": "Invariant Violation",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0053. Formal verification of invariant across all execution paths to ensure no bypass exists."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "property_id": "PROP-0054",
      "status": "success",
      "filtering_summary": {
        "total_properties_input": 1,
        "filtered_out_of_scope": 0,
        "filtered_api_only": 0,
        "filtered_configuration_error": 0,
        "properties_processed": 1
      },
      "checklist": [
        {
          "check_id": "CHK-0054-01",
          "property_id": "PROP-0054",
          "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is block number validated to equal parent.number + 1 at P2P ingress?",
          "severity": "High",
          "mindset": "Boundary Guard",
          "is_boundary_check": true,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Identify all P2P block handlers. 2. Trace parent block lookup and number comparison. 3. Verify validation occurs BEFORE any state modifications. 4. Submit block with number = parent.number + 1 - verify acceptance. 5. Submit block with number = parent.number (same as parent) - verify rejection. 6. Submit block with number = parent.number + 2 (skip) - verify rejection. 7. Submit block with number = 0 - verify rejection. 8. Submit block with number = MAX_UINT64 to test overflow handling. 9. Test with orphan blocks (unknown parent) - verify safe handling.",
          "bug_class": "Consensus Validation",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0054, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Subgraph: SG-004. Incorrect block number breaks chain continuity and could cause state indexing errors."
        },
        {
          "check_id": "CHK-0054-02",
          "property_id": "PROP-0054",
          "title": "Falsify Invariant: Can block processing accept a block with number != parent.number + 1?",
          "severity": "High",
          "mindset": "Formal Verification Engineer",
          "is_boundary_check": false,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Enumerate all block insertion paths (P2P, import, reorg, snap sync). 2. Verify each path validates block.number == parent.number + 1. 3. Check for arithmetic overflow in parent.number + 1 computation. 4. Check for bypass conditions (genesis block, checkpoint blocks, etc.). 5. Test concurrent block insertions with conflicting numbers. 6. Verify block number is stored and retrieved consistently across restarts.",
          "bug_class": "Invariant Violation",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0054. Falsification test to ensure block number invariant cannot be violated through any path."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "property_id": "PROP-0055",
      "status": "success",
      "filtering_summary": {
        "total_properties_input": 1,
        "filtered_out_of_scope": 0,
        "filtered_api_only": 0,
        "filtered_configuration_error": 0,
        "properties_processed": 1
      },
      "checklist": [
        {
          "check_id": "CHK-0055-01",
          "property_id": "PROP-0055",
          "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the state_root in block headers correctly validated against computed state after transaction execution?",
          "severity": "Critical",
          "mindset": "Boundary Guard",
          "is_boundary_check": true,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Identify all code paths where blocks are received via P2P network. 2. Trace the block validation logic to locate state_root verification. 3. Verify that state_root in the header is compared against the computed state root AFTER all transactions are executed. 4. Attempt to submit a block with a valid transaction set but incorrect state_root - verify rejection. 5. Attempt to submit a block with modified state_root matching a different valid state - verify rejection. 6. Check for timing windows where state_root validation could be bypassed. 7. Verify error handling ensures block is fully rejected (not partially processed) on mismatch.",
          "bug_class": "State Consistency",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0055, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Subgraph: SG-004. Post-condition property ensuring consensus integrity. A bypass could allow attackers to inject blocks with invalid state transitions."
        },
        {
          "check_id": "CHK-0055-02",
          "property_id": "PROP-0055",
          "title": "Falsify Post-Condition: Can block processing complete successfully with mismatched state_root?",
          "severity": "Critical",
          "mindset": "Formal Verification Engineer",
          "is_boundary_check": false,
          "reachability": {
            "classification": "external-reachable",
            "entry_points": ["P2P"],
            "attacker_controlled": true,
            "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope"
          },
          "test_procedure": "1. Create a test block with valid transactions but deliberately compute an incorrect state_root. 2. Submit the block through the P2P interface. 3. Verify the block is rejected with appropriate error code. 4. Check that no state changes from the block's transactions persist after rejection. 5. Verify the node's state remains consistent with the pre-submission state. 6. Test edge cases: empty block with wrong state_root, block with single transaction and wrong state_root, block at state trie boundary conditions.",
          "bug_class": "Consensus Violation",
          "risk_category": "Tampering",
          "notes": "Source: PROP-0055. Falsification test for post-condition: After transaction execution, state_root MUST match header. Failure to enforce this invariant would allow consensus divergence."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_items": 3,
    "successful": 3,
    "failed": 0,
    "total_checks_generated": 6,
    "by_severity": {
      "Critical": 2,
      "High": 2,
      "Medium": 2
    },
    "by_mindset": {
      "Boundary Guard": 3,
      "Formal Verification Engineer": 3
    },
    "all_in_scope": true
  },

    "worker_id": 6,

  }
}

+          "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the extra_data field in block headers properly validated to not exceed 32 bytes at P2P ingress?",

+          "test_procedure": "1. Identify all P2P block reception handlers. 2. Trace block header deserialization to locate extra_data field extraction. 3. Verify length validation occurs BEFORE any processing of extra_data content. 4. Submit block with extra_data of exactly 32 bytes - verify acceptance. 5. Submit block with extra_data of 33 bytes - verify rejection. 6. Submit block with extra_data of 0 bytes - verify handling. 7. Submit block with extra_data at maximum uint64 length value - verify safe rejection without buffer overflow. 8. Verify error response does not leak internal state. 9. Check that validation occurs at the earliest possible point in the processing pipeline.",
+          "notes": "Source: PROP-0053, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Subgraph: SG-004. Invariant property for extra_data length. Oversized extra_data could cause buffer issues or consensus splits."
+          "title": "Is the 32-byte extra_data length invariant maintained consistently across all block validation paths?",

+          "test_procedure": "1. Enumerate all code paths that process block headers (P2P, RPC, local block production, import). 2. Verify each path enforces the 32-byte limit. 3. Check for bypass conditions (special block types, genesis block, etc.). 4. Verify the comparison operator is correct (<=32, not <32 or <33). 5. Check for integer overflow in length comparisons. 6. Test blocks created through alternative paths (snap sync, fast sync, etc.).",
+          "notes": "Source: PROP-0053. Formal verification of invariant across all execution paths to ensure no bypass exists."
+          "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is block number validated to equal parent.number + 1 at P2P ingress?",

+          "test_procedure": "1. Identify all P2P block handlers. 2. Trace parent block lookup and number comparison. 3. Verify validation occurs BEFORE any state modifications. 4. Submit block with number = parent.number + 1 - verify acceptance. 5. Submit block with number = parent.number (same as parent) - verify rejection. 6. Submit block with number = parent.number + 2 (skip) - verify rejection. 7. Submit block with number = 0 - verify rejection. 8. Submit block with number = MAX_UINT64 to test overflow handling. 9. Test with orphan blocks (unknown parent) - verify safe handling.",
+          "notes": "Source: PROP-0054, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Subgraph: SG-004. Incorrect block number breaks chain continuity and could cause state indexing errors."
+          "title": "Falsify Invariant: Can block processing accept a block with number != parent.number + 1?",

+          "test_procedure": "1. Enumerate all block insertion paths (P2P, import, reorg, snap sync). 2. Verify each path validates block.number == parent.number + 1. 3. Check for arithmetic overflow in parent.number + 1 computation. 4. Check for bypass conditions (genesis block, checkpoint blocks, etc.). 5. Test concurrent block insertions with conflicting numbers. 6. Verify block number is stored and retrieved consistently across restarts.",
